text: A search system should not only match similar words. If the user asks for the conclusion, the system should rank posts with matching conclusions higher.
The site is slow because every page loads too many unrelated assets. Reducing unnecessary assets should improve response time.
A definition is useful when terms are ambiguous. In this experiment, structure reranking means changing the order of baseline search results using extracted reasoning structures.
Vector search can find topical similarity, but it may confuse formatting similarity with argument similarity. Structure extraction can separate those signals.
This approach is not a replacement for dense retrieval. It is a small reranking layer that tests whether conclusions, premises, causes, contrasts, and definitions improve search.
Markdown lists often look similar even when the ideas are unrelated. Formatting should not dominate the search score when the query asks about reasoning.
If a post claims that public experiments improve trust, and another post argues the opposite from the same premise, both should be discoverable for comparison.
A causal explanation connects a reason to an outcome. For example, if extraction errors are frequent, reranking can become worse.
